scenes.scene_name: The Simile of the Flooding Banks
scenes.summary: Dante uses a simile comparing the souls to people building defenses against floods in Flanders and Padua to describe the formation of the approaching group.
Dante describes how the travelers become lost in the woods before encountering a group of souls watching them intensely.
A member of the group recognizes Dante by his clothing and calls out to him.
Brunetto Latino greets Dante as his son and tells him to keep moving ahead while he catches up later.
Dante explains how he became lost in the dark wood and ended up on this path.
Brunetto reassures Dante that if he follows his destiny, he will reach a glorious port.
Brunetto warns Dante that the people of Fiesole will be his enemies due to their greedy and proud nature.

scenes.scene_name: The Prophecy of the Holy Seed
scenes.summary: Brunetto predicts that while the Fiesolese will vie for Dante, their malice will not destroy the sacred legacy of the Romans.
Dante expresses his deep gratitude and emotional connection to Brunetto's past teachings.
Dante vows to record this encounter and expresses his stoic acceptance of the whims of Fortune.
Virgil turns back to Dante and tells him to listen closely to what is being noted.
Dante asks Brunetto about the identities of the other notable souls in the procession.
Brunetto identifies several famous scholars who are in Hell due to the same sin.
Brunetto is forced to end the conversation because a new group of souls is approaching.
Dante concludes the canto with a simile comparing the new group of souls to victorious runners in Verona.
